summary: Completed testing of Notification Preferences UI in Settings page and Benevolence PDF Export feature. All 11 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend tests passed for notification toggles and PDF export functionality. Fixed a minor bug in BenevolencePage.js where the auth token was being retrieved from wrong localStorage key.

minor.issue: Response does not merge defaults with stored preferences - only returns stored fields. If user has only updated some preferences, the others are missing from response. Frontend works around this by setting defaults in component state.

issues: Console warning: Switch component changing from controlled to uncontrolled - minor React anti-pattern, non-blocking

backend_api: GET /api/notifications/preferences returns user preferences with user_id
PUT /api/notifications/preferences can update multiple fields at once
GET /api/benevolence/export/{trust_id}/pdf generates valid PDF with correct headers
Notification preference persistence verified (Create → GET verification)
frontend_ui: Notification Preferences section visible in Settings page (data-testid='notification-preferences-section')
Minutes Created toggle (notif-minutes-created) visible and functional
Task Reminders toggle (notif-task-reminders) visible and functional
Weekly Digest toggle (notif-weekly-digest) visible and functional
Toggle interaction works - state changes when clicked
Benevolence page loads with Export Report button visible
Export Report button (data-testid='export-benevolence-pdf') triggers PDF download
Success toast 'Report downloaded' appears after export

critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: BenevolencePage.js handleExportPDF was using wrong localStorage key ('token' instead of 'auth_token'), causing 401 errors on PDF export. Fixed by using correct key and adding credentials:include
Minor: GET /api/notifications/preferences should merge default values with stored values for complete response. Currently only returns user_id + any stored preferences.
Minor: Switch component in SettingsPage shows React warning about controlled/uncontrolled - should initialize with explicit values from loaded prefs
seed_data_creation: Used 'Use Demo Data' button to create Smith Family Trust with benevolence_enabled=true
context_for_next_testing_agent: Notification Preferences and Benevolence PDF Export features are fully functional. The Settings page has 7 notification toggle switches that save changes in real-time via PUT /api/notifications/preferences. The Benevolence page has an Export Report button that downloads a styled PDF report grouped by purpose category. Both features are using the trust 'Smith Family Trust' (trust_f8896488ce03) which has benevolence_enabled=true.

benevolence_pdf_export.pdf_content: Styled PDF with trust info, summary statistics, and grants grouped by purpose category

bug_fixed.issue: handleExportPDF was using localStorage.getItem('token') but app stores token as 'auth_token'. Also was missing credentials:include for cookie auth.
bug_fixed.fix: Changed to use auth_token key with fallback to session_token cookie, added credentials:include
